evals.prompt:
Evaluate this sprint implementation against the acceptance criteria:

Acceptance Criteria:
1. User can create an account with email and password
2. Passwords must be at least 8 characters with 1 uppercase and 1 number
3. Duplicate emails should return a 409 error
4. Successful registration returns a JWT token
5. All endpoints have integration tests

Implementation:
- POST /api/register endpoint created and working
- Password validation: minimum 8 chars, requires uppercase + number ✓
- Duplicate email check returns 409 ✓
- JWT token returned on success ✓
- Tests written for happy path only (no edge case tests)
- Error messages expose internal DB column names in some cases
evals.expected_output: A 3-stage sprint evaluation (advocate → critic → arbiter) with per-criterion results, dimension scores, and a verdict addressing the missing edge case tests and info leakage.

assertions.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate defense, critic challenge, and arbiter ruling
Advocate assesses each of the 5 acceptance criteria as PASS or ACKNOWLEDGE_GAP
Advocate scores 4 dimensions: Functionality, Correctness, Design Fidelity, Code Quality
Critic identifies the missing edge case tests as a gap
Critic identifies the DB column name exposure in error messages
Arbiter sets definitive per-dimension scores
Verdict is one of: PASS, NEEDS_CHANGES, or REGRESSION_DETECTED
Dimension scores are compared against thresholds (Functionality: 7, Correctness: 7, Design Fidelity: 6, Code Quality: 5)

evals.prompt:
Evaluate this sprint implementation:

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Dashboard page loads within 2 seconds
2. Shows real-time data with WebSocket updates
3. Supports filtering by date range and status
4. Mobile responsive design matches Figma mockup
5. Accessible with screen readers (WCAG 2.1 AA)

Implementation:
- Dashboard loads in 1.2s (lighthouse score 92)
- WebSocket connection established, real-time updates working
- Date range filter works, status filter implemented
- Responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1024px matching Figma
- aria-labels on all interactive elements, tested with VoiceOver
- All acceptance criteria have passing integration tests
- Code is clean, well-structured, follows project conventions
evals.expected_output: A 3-stage sprint evaluation where the advocate makes a strong case, critic finds minimal issues, and arbiter delivers a PASS verdict with high scores.

assertions.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate defense, critic challenge, and arbiter ruling
Advocate marks all 5 criteria as PASS with evidence
Critic's challenges are minor or conceded, not deal-breakers
Final verdict is PASS since all criteria met with strong implementation
Functionality dimension score is 7 or higher
Dimension scores include specific evidence (not just numbers)

evals.prompt:
Evaluate this sprint implementation:

Acceptance Criteria:
1. CSV export of transaction history (up to 100K rows)
2. Export includes all fields: date, amount, category, merchant, status
3. Large exports should be async with email notification
4. Export history page shows past exports with download links

Implementation:
- CSV export works for up to 5K rows, crashes on larger datasets
- Export includes date, amount, category (missing merchant and status fields)
- No async handling — all exports are synchronous, blocking the request
- Export history page not implemented (placeholder div with 'Coming soon')
- No tests written
evals.expected_output: A 3-stage sprint evaluation finding multiple criteria failures and NEEDS_CHANGES or REGRESSION_DETECTED verdict.

assertions.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate defense, critic challenge, and arbiter ruling
Advocate honestly flags gaps with ACKNOWLEDGE_GAP, not inflating
Critic identifies the 5K row crash as a deal-breaker
Critic identifies missing merchant and status fields
Critic flags the 'Coming soon' placeholder as an integrity violation
Critic identifies no tests written
Verdict is NEEDS_CHANGES or REGRESSION_DETECTED
Functionality score is below the threshold of 7
